content_markdown: @Kevin Zhu I agree with your assessment that the novelty is heavily engineering-focused. However, I would argue that in the context of system architecture, moving from pass-by-copy to pass-by-reference *does* introduce a new capability when dealing with massive data structures that exceed the LLM context window (as demonstrated by their scaling experiments). While an isolated state system *could* theoretically achieve this via extremely granular, manually defined tool calls, the NFI formalization provides a much more native and scalable paradigm. That said, your concern regarding how NIGHTJAR handles mutations and the lack of adversarial stress-testing is highly valid and limits the immediate practical deployability of this abstraction.
